q: What’s the best time to visit Bali?
a: Bali’s dry season runs from April to October. Shoulder months like May and September offer sunny days and fewer crowds.

q: Do I need a visa to enter Indonesia/Bali?
a: Many nationalities are eligible for Visa on Arrival (VOA) or e-VOA for stays up to 30 days, extendable once. Always check official government sources for the latest rules.

q: How do I get around Bali?
a: Private driver is the most comfortable. Ride-hailing apps are available in many areas. Scooters are common but ride only if experienced and always wear a helmet.

q: Is Bali safe for solo travelers and families?
a: Generally yes. Use common sense, keep valuables secure, and follow local advice. Beaches and attractions are family-friendly.

q: What should I wear when visiting temples?
a: Cover shoulders and knees; a sarong is often required. Many temples provide sarongs at the entrance.

q: What’s the cancellation and refund policy?
a: Free cancellation up to 48 hours before the tour. 50% fee within 48–24 hours; no refund within 24 hours or no-shows.

q: What currency is used and can I pay by card?
a: Indonesian Rupiah (IDR). Cards are widely accepted in tourist areas; ATMs are common. We also accept bank transfer and cash.

q: Do I need a SIM card or will Wi-Fi be enough?
a: Local SIM/eSIM plans are affordable with good coverage in tourist zones. Most hotels and cafés offer reliable Wi-Fi.
